summary_one_line: Buffer must shape as a Devanagari 0x0905 base followed by a second codepoint in the 0x0946-0x094B vowel set so the inner switch case matches.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A glyph buffer routed to HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI whose current codepoint is 0x0905 and whose next codepoint (buffer->cur(1)) equals one of 0x0946/0x0949/0x094A/0x094B, so the inner switch on cur(1).codepoint takes the true arm at line 68.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile wins 8/10 and reaches the branch true side (avg hitcount 9 vs 0); its CMP_MAP gradient steadily preserves inputs whose CMP operands inch toward the required Devanagari codepoint constants, and the resolving seed carries 09 09 05 09 byte runs consistent with the 0x0905/0x094X 16-bit codepoints driving the switch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile_cmplog resolves only 2/10 (avg hitcount 0); it pays I2SRandReplace per-execution cost and corpus churn on top of the same CMP_MAP gradient, and its blocking seeds stay on the false arm (line 68 L counts T=0/T=1 F=17/F=18) without assembling the 0x0905-then-0x094X pair.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers share the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix distance feedback) that incrementally rewards inputs whose codepoint CMP operands approach the case constants; value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog because the winner relies on that gradient unburdened, whereas adding the I2S input-to-state substitution mutator (the single technique delta) does not help solve this nested 16-bit-codepoint switch and its overhead/corpus churn dilutes the loser's progress.

evidence_trail.claim: The only decisive pair is value_profile > value_profile_cmplog with technique delta I2S.
The blocker is the inner switch case on the second Devanagari codepoint, line 68, true side globally blocked.
Matching in this shaper depends on buffer->cur(1).codepoint, the second glyph's codepoint, being a specific Devanagari constant.
value_profile resolves the branch 8/10 with average hitcount 9 while value_profile_cmplog resolves 2/10 with average hitcount 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=9  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving value_profile seed carries 0x0905/0x094X-consistent codepoint byte runs (09 09 05 09).
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and is driven by the CMP_MAP gradient that preserves inputs whose CMP operands approach a match.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the same edge plus CMP_MAP machinery the winner already has.
The CMP_MAP gradient preserves an input whose CMP-operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds stay on the false arm at the blocker line (true counts 0/1 vs large false counts).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L  68  T=0 F=1  T=1 F=17  case 0x0946u: case 0x0949u: case 0x094Au: case 0x094Bu:  <-- BLOCKER

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (not I2SRandReplace), so lineage is not required, but I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6010 --role W --fuzzer value_profile --trial 2 --seed b66b6d7aad45d988` for corroboration: the winner chain is havoc/token-dominated (DwordAddMutator/WordAddMutator/TokenReplace etc.) consistent with a CMP_MAP-guided, non-I2S winner; one dash row appears at depth 32, which I treat as cross-fuzzer corpus-inheritance noise rather than evidence that the value_profile winner used I2S, since the winning mechanism is the shared gradient, not the I2S delta.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same nested 16-bit-codepoint Devanagari switch in which value_profile_cmplog resolves the case as fast as or faster than value_profile across trials, showing that adding I2SRandReplace does not hurt and the CMP_MAP gradient is not the decisive advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level link from the winner seed's 09 09 05 09 run to the specific 0x0905/0x094X codepoints is inferred from glyph-codepoint plausibility, not from a confirmed input-to-codepoint decode path in this harness.
